question.text: PAN is mandatory for all mutual fund investments. Which of the following is NOT an exemption from the PAN requirement?

answer.text: Micro-SIPs up to Rs.50,000 per year per mutual fund
Investments by senior citizens above 70 years of age

explanation.text: **The Logic:** PAN exemptions are limited to: (1) Micro-SIPs up to Rs.50,000/year/MF plus lump sum up to Rs.50,000/year/MF (composite limit), (2) Residents of Sikkim, (3) UN entities, and (4) Government undertakings. Senior citizens, regardless of age, are NOT exempt from PAN requirements for mutual fund investments. **The Trap:** Senior citizens get exemptions in several financial contexts (higher interest rates, tax benefits), so candidates may assume PAN exemption extends to them too. It does not. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers PAN exemptions; Series II-B tests the RTA's verification process for PAN-exempt categories. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When onboarding PAN-exempt investors (Micro-SIP, Sikkim residents), ensure they obtain a PEKRN (PAN Exempt KYC Reference Number) from the KRA. This number substitutes for PAN in all transaction processing.
